utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items requested in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 items appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: 1) health_coverage: requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan lists separate physical and mental plans with explicit monthly costs and add-ons, so fully specified. 2) property_coverage: requires home/contents insurance with costs. The plan includes home content insurance with a monthly cost and an extra emergency response service with cost, so specified. 3) travel_coverage: requires travel insurance with costs. The plan includes a travel insurance plan with a monthly cost and an assistance add-on with cost, so specified. 4) additional_services: requires any enhancement services with costs. The plan includes an 'Insurance Portfolio Management' service with a €300 one-time cost; although other text discusses offsets conceptually, there is at least one concrete service with cost, so meets requirement. 5) total_budget: requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. The plan provides monthly and annual totals, so specified. Therefore, all 5/5 items are included and fully specified according to the format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: I matched each item named in the final coverage plan to the exact corresponding entry in the ground-truth ratings. For primary plans (health, mental health, home content, travel), I used the plan names exactly. For add-ons/enhancements, I mapped to the identically named services under health_enhancement_services, property_enhancement_services, and travel_enhancement_services. The 'Insurance Portfolio Management' is a general service with a one-time cost and has a rating provided.
